guidelines_title: NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Testicular Cancer

changes: Initial testicular/GCT taxonomy built from NCCN Testicular Cancer v1.2026 (95 pages). Covers SEM-1 through SEM-8 and NSEM-1 through NSEM-10.
17 disease categories spanning pure seminoma and nonseminomatous GCT (NSGCT) across stage, IGCCCG risk, and treatment-line axes.
9 clinical axes: histology, clinical_stage, igcccg_risk, primary_site, prior_chemo_lines, prior_hdct, rplnd_status, marker_status, stage1_risk_factors.
Regimen reference tables embedded: BEP, EP, TIP, VeIP, TI-CE (HD), carboplatin AUC7, gemcitabine combinations.
IGCCCG risk model (2021 Gillessen update) embedded with nomogram reference.
Extragonadal GCT category with high-volume center referral note.

classification_philosophy: Inclusive — when ambiguous, include in adjacent categories. Multi-label: one trial may span stage I and advanced settings. Histology-aware: seminoma and NSGCT have separate treatment algorithms; trials restricted to one histology must not be multi-labeled to the other.

categories.label: Seminoma — Stage I (IA/IB/IS)
categories.description: Pure seminoma, clinical stage IA/IB/IS after radical inguinal orchiectomy. Primary treatment: surveillance (strongly preferred), adjuvant carboplatin (AUC=7, 1-2 cycles), or adjuvant RT (minority). Stage IS: repeat markers and imaging.

categories.label: Seminoma — Stage IIA/IIB
categories.description: Pure seminoma, clinical stage IIA or IIB. Radiation therapy (preferred for IIA, option for IIB) or first-line chemotherapy (BEP x3 or EP x4). RPLND option for select IIA with normal markers.

categories.label: Seminoma — Stage IIC/III (Advanced)
categories.description: Pure seminoma, advanced (stage IIC/III). IGCCCG risk-stratified first-line chemotherapy: good risk → BEP x3 or EP x4; intermediate risk (non-pulmonary visceral mets) → BEP x4. No IGCCCG poor risk category for seminoma.

categories.label: Seminoma — Post First-Line Chemo Management
categories.description: Seminoma after first-line chemotherapy: CR → surveillance; residual ≤3 cm → surveillance or FDG-PET; residual >3 cm → FDG-PET to guide further management.

categories.label: Seminoma — Recurrent / Second-Line
categories.description: Recurrent or refractory seminoma after first-line chemotherapy. Conventional-dose second-line (VeIP x4 or TIP x4) or high-dose chemotherapy (preferred at experienced centers). Clinical trial strongly encouraged.

categories.label: Seminoma — Third-Line and Beyond
categories.description: Seminoma, third-line and beyond. Clinical trial preferred. High-dose chemotherapy (if not previously received), gemcitabine combinations, or molecular targeted agents for actionable mutations.

categories.label: NSGCT — Stage I (IA/IB, with/without Risk Factors)
categories.description: NSGCT (includes mixed GCT with nonseminoma elements), clinical stage IA or IB. Treatment based on presence/absence of risk factors (LVI, spermatic cord or scrotal invasion). Options: surveillance (preferred), nerve-sparing RPLND, or adjuvant BEP x1 cycle.

categories.label: NSGCT — Stage IS (Persistently Elevated Markers)
categories.description: NSGCT, stage IS: persistently elevated or rising tumor markers (AFP, beta-hCG) after orchiectomy without radiographic evidence of disease. Treat with first-line chemotherapy (BEP x3 or EP x4).

categories.label: NSGCT — Stage IIA/IIB
categories.description: NSGCT stage IIA or IIB. Primary nerve-sparing RPLND (preferred for IIA with negative markers) or first-line chemotherapy (BEP x3 or EP x4). Stage IIB: chemotherapy preferred.

categories.label: NSGCT — Stage IIC/III, IGCCCG Good Risk
categories.description: NSGCT/NSGCT, advanced (stage IIC/III), IGCCCG good risk: testicular/retroperitoneal primary, no non-pulmonary visceral mets, AFP <1000, hCG <5000, LDH <1.5×ULN. BEP x3 (preferred) or EP x4.

categories.label: NSGCT — Stage IIC/III, IGCCCG Intermediate/Poor Risk
categories.description: NSGCT, advanced (stage IIC/III), IGCCCG intermediate or poor risk. Intermediate: testicular/retroperitoneal primary, no non-pulmonary visceral mets, elevated markers mid-range. Poor: mediastinal primary OR non-pulmonary visceral mets OR AFP >10000 OR hCG >50000 OR LDH >10×ULN. BEP x4 or VIP x4.

categories.label: NSGCT — Post First-Line Chemo Management / PC-RPLND
categories.description: NSGCT after first-line chemotherapy. Complete response → surveillance or RPLND (selected). Residual masses → PC-RPLND (surgery for all residual). Pathology: necrosis/fibrosis → surveillance; teratoma → surveillance; viable GCT → 2 additional cycles.

categories.label: NSGCT — Recurrent / Second-Line
categories.description: Recurrent or refractory NSGCT after first-line chemotherapy. Conventional-dose second-line (TIP x4 or VeIP x4) or high-dose chemotherapy with stem cell rescue (TI-CE). Clinical trial strongly encouraged. High-volume center referral recommended.

categories.label: NSGCT — Third-Line and Beyond / Post-HDCT
categories.description: NSGCT/GCT, third-line and beyond, or after prior high-dose chemotherapy. Clinical trial preferred. High-dose chemo (if not previously received), gemcitabine combinations (GemOx, GemOxP, GemPaclitaxel). Molecular/pan-cancer agents for actionable mutations.

categories.label: GCT — Stage I (Any Histology / Unspecified)
categories.description: Testicular GCT, stage I, any or unspecified histology — use when trial does not restrict to seminoma or NSGCT only. Multi-label with seminoma_stage1 or nsgct_stage1 when histology is specified.

categories.label: GCT — Advanced / Metastatic (Any Histology / Unspecified)
categories.description: Advanced or metastatic GCT, any or unspecified histology — use when trial enrolls both seminoma and NSGCT. Multi-label with histology-specific categories when applicable.

categories.label: Extragonadal GCT (Mediastinal / Retroperitoneal Primary)
categories.description: Primary extragonadal GCT — mediastinal (primary mediastinal NSGCT is an IGCCCG poor-risk factor) or retroperitoneal origin. High-volume center referral strongly recommended. Mediastinal primary NSGCT = IGCCCG poor risk regardless of markers.

histology.description: Tumor histology — the primary treatment-determining axis. Pure seminoma and nonseminoma (NSGCT) have completely separate treatment pathways. Mixed GCT with any nonseminomatous elements is treated as NSGCT.
histology._ui_note: If histology=unknown: surface both seminoma and NSGCT trials with flag 'Confirm histology — pure seminoma vs nonseminoma (NSGCT) determines the entire treatment pathway including first-line regimen, RT eligibility, and post-chemo management. AFP elevation in a seminoma histology reclassifies the patient as NSGCT.'

clinical_stage.description: AJCC 8th edition clinical stage after orchiectomy (post-orchiectomy serum tumor markers used for S-stage). Stage I = no nodal or distant mets; Stage IS = persistently elevated markers; Stage IIA/IIB = retroperitoneal lymph node enlargement ≤5 cm; Stage IIC = retroperitoneal LN >5 cm; Stage III = distant mets.
clinical_stage._ui_note: If clinical_stage=unknown: surface all stage-relevant trials with flag 'Confirm post-orchiectomy clinical stage — imaging and serum tumor markers (AFP, beta-hCG, LDH) needed to assign AJCC stage and IGCCCG risk group.'

igcccg_risk.description: IGCCCG (International Germ-Cell Cancer Collaborative Group) risk classification for advanced/metastatic GCT. Applied post-orchiectomy on Day 1 of chemotherapy. Note: IGCCCG does not include a poor-risk category for pure seminoma — seminoma is either good or intermediate risk.
igcccg_risk._ui_note: If igcccg_risk=unknown and trial restricts to a specific risk group: surface trial with flag 'IGCCCG risk classification not confirmed — calculate using AFP, beta-hCG, LDH measured on Day 1 of chemotherapy, primary site, and presence of non-pulmonary visceral metastases.'

primary_site.description: Primary tumor location — testicular (gonadal) vs extragonadal. Mediastinal primary is a key determinant of IGCCCG poor risk for NSGCT regardless of marker levels.
primary_site._ui_note: If primary_site=unknown and trial requires mediastinal or extragonadal primary: surface trial with flag 'Confirm primary tumor site — mediastinal primary NSGCT is classified as IGCCCG poor risk regardless of tumor markers.'

prior_chemo_lines.description: Number of prior chemotherapy regimens for GCT (not counting adjuvant single-agent carboplatin for seminoma as a 'line').
prior_chemo_lines._ui_note: If prior_chemo_lines=unknown and trial targets a specific treatment line: surface trial with flag 'Confirm number of prior chemotherapy regimens — GCT trials are strictly line-defined (1st-line BEP/EP, 2nd-line TIP/VeIP, 3rd-line HD-chemo).'

prior_hdct.description: Prior high-dose chemotherapy (HDCT) with stem cell rescue (e.g., TI-CE, carboplatin/etoposide HD). Determines eligibility for further HDCT vs conventional-dose salvage.
prior_hdct._ui_note: If prior_hdct=unknown and trial restricts by HDCT history: surface trial with flag 'Confirm prior high-dose chemotherapy history — some salvage regimens (TI-CE) are only eligible for patients who have not previously received HDCT.'

rplnd_status.description: Whether retroperitoneal lymph node dissection (RPLND) has been performed — primary (diagnostic/therapeutic) or post-chemotherapy (PC-RPLND).
rplnd_status._ui_note: If rplnd_status=unknown and trial requires or excludes RPLND: surface trial with flag 'Confirm whether retroperitoneal lymph node dissection has been performed — primary RPLND and post-chemotherapy RPLND have distinct eligibility implications.'

marker_status.description: Post-orchiectomy serum tumor marker status (AFP, beta-hCG, LDH). Critical for staging (S-classification) and IGCCCG risk. AFP elevation in seminoma histology reclassifies as NSGCT.
marker_status._ui_note: If marker_status=unknown and trial uses markers for eligibility or risk stratification: surface trial with flag 'Confirm serum tumor marker levels (AFP, beta-hCG, LDH) — these are required for IGCCCG risk classification and AJCC S-staging.'

stage1_risk_factors.description: Risk factors for recurrence in stage I NSGCT: lymphovascular invasion (LVI) or invasion of spermatic cord or scrotum. Determines treatment decision in stage I NSGCT (surveillance vs RPLND vs adjuvant BEP x1).
stage1_risk_factors._ui_note: If stage1_risk_factors=unknown and trial stratifies stage I NSGCT by risk: surface trial with flag 'Confirm lymphovascular invasion (LVI) and spermatic cord/scrotal invasion status — these risk factors determine surveillance vs RPLND vs adjuvant BEP x1 in stage I NSGCT.'

seminoma_stage1: Adjuvant carboplatin AUC=7 x1 cycle or AUC=7 x2 cycles
Adjuvant radiation therapy (minority — stage I only)
seminoma_stage2a_2b: Radiation therapy (preferred stage IIA; option IIB)
BEP x3 cycles or EP x4 cycles (first-line chemo)
Nerve-sparing RPLND (select stage IIA, normal markers)
seminoma_stage2c_3: BEP x3 cycles (good risk) or EP x4 cycles (good risk alternative)
BEP x4 cycles (intermediate risk — non-pulmonary visceral mets)
seminoma_recurrent_2l: High-dose chemotherapy with stem cell rescue (preferred)
seminoma_3l_plus: High-dose chemotherapy if not previously received
nsgct_stage_is: BEP x3 cycles or EP x4 cycles (first-line chemotherapy)
nsgct_stage2a_2b: Nerve-sparing RPLND (preferred stage IIA, negative markers)
BEP x3 cycles or EP x4 cycles (stage IIA marker+; stage IIB preferred)
nsgct_post_first_line: 2 additional cycles EP/TIP/VIP/VeIP (residual viable GCT on pathology)
nsgct_recurrent_2l: TI-CE high-dose chemotherapy (preferred overall at experienced centers)
extragonadal_gct: BEP x3-4 cycles per risk group

known_trial_anchors.description: Named landmark trials for cross-reference and classification validation

trials.name: TE19
trials.intervention: Carboplatin AUC=7 vs radiation therapy, stage I seminoma

trials.name: SWENOTECA surveillance
trials.intervention: Surveillance vs adjuvant therapy, stage I seminoma

trials.name: TIGR / TIGER
trials.intervention: TI-CE high-dose chemo vs TIP conventional-dose, relapsed GCT

trials.name: TI-CE protocol
trials.intervention: High-dose carboplatin/etoposide x2 cycles with stem cell rescue

trials.name: IGCCCG 2021 update
trials.intervention: Updated prognostic model (Gillessen et al., JCO 2021) — nomogram at eortc.shinyapps.io/IGCCCG-Update

igcccg_risk_model.description: International Germ-Cell Cancer Collaborative Group (IGCCCG) risk classification — applied post-orchiectomy, using Day 1 of first-line chemotherapy marker values. Updated nomogram: Gillessen S, et al. J Clin Oncol 2021;39:1563-1574.

good_risk.criteria: Any primary site, non-mediastinal origin, no non-pulmonary visceral metastases, any AFP, any LDH, any hCG

intermediate_risk.criteria: Any primary site, non-mediastinal origin, non-pulmonary visceral metastases (liver, bone, brain, other)

seminoma.poor_risk: Not applicable — IGCCCG does not define poor risk for seminoma

good_risk.criteria: Testicular/retroperitoneal primary AND no non-pulmonary visceral mets AND AFP <1000 AND hCG <5000 AND LDH <1.5×ULN

intermediate_risk.criteria: Testicular/retroperitoneal primary AND no non-pulmonary visceral mets AND [AFP 1000-10000 OR hCG 5000-50000 OR LDH 1.5-10×ULN]

poor_risk.criteria: Mediastinal primary OR non-pulmonary visceral mets OR AFP >10000 OR hCG >50000 OR LDH >10×ULN

first_line.BEP: Bleomycin 30 units IV weekly (D1,8,15) + Etoposide 100 mg/m2 IV D1-5 + Cisplatin 20 mg/m2 IV D1-5; q21d. Good risk x3 cycles; intermediate/poor risk x4 cycles.
first_line.EP: Etoposide 100 mg/m2 IV D1-5 + Cisplatin 20 mg/m2 IV D1-5; q21d x4 cycles. Good risk only. Bleomycin-free alternative.
first_line.VIP: Etoposide 75 mg/m2 IV D1-5 + Ifosfamide 1200 mg/m2 IV D1-5 + Cisplatin 20 mg/m2 IV D1-5; q21d. Alternative to BEP for intermediate/poor risk.
first_line.carboplatin_AUC7: Carboplatin AUC=7 x1 or x2 cycles. Adjuvant for stage I seminoma only.

second_line.TIP: Paclitaxel 250 mg/m2 IV D1 + Ifosfamide 1500 mg/m2 IV D2-5 + Cisplatin 25 mg/m2 IV D2-5; q21d x4 cycles.
second_line.VeIP: Vinblastine 0.11 mg/kg IV D1-2 + Ifosfamide 1200 mg/m2 IV D1-5 + Cisplatin 20 mg/m2 IV D1-5; q21d x4 cycles.
second_line.TI_CE_HD: TI induction: Paclitaxel + Ifosfamide x2 cycles, then CE high-dose: Carboplatin AUC 8 D-5 to D-3 + Etoposide 400 mg/m2 D-5 to D-3; x3 cycles with autologous PBSC rescue (Feldman 2010).

third_line.carbo_etop_HD: Carboplatin 700 mg/m2/day IV D-5,-4,-3 + Etoposide 750 mg/m2/day IV D-5,-4,-3; x2 cycles with PBSC rescue.
third_line.PICE_HD: Paclitaxel + Ifosfamide + Carboplatin + Etoposide (high-dose), x2 cycles with PBSC rescue.
third_line.GemOxP: Gemcitabine 800 mg/m2 + Paclitaxel 80 mg/m2 IV D1,8 + Oxaliplatin 130 mg/m2 IV D1; q21d x8 cycles.
third_line.GemOx: Gemcitabine 1000-1250 mg/m2 IV D1,8 + Oxaliplatin 130 mg/m2 IV D1; q21d until progression.
